text: The surgeon begins the low anterior resection by mobilizing the sigmoid colon from medial to lateral using monopolar electrocautery. The ureter is identified and preserved, confirmed with ICG technology. A powered stapler is used to ligate the inferior mesenteric vein, followed by high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery, which is clipped twice proximally and once distally. A bipolar energy device cauterizes and cuts the IMA from the base of the aorta.

The surgeon begins the total mesorectal excision anteriorly between the rectum and the vagina, then continues posteriorly and laterally in the avascular plane. The dissection is carried down to the level of the levator muscles, where the rectum is completely circumferentially dissected. A blue load, sixty-five powered stapler is used to transect the rectum, requiring multiple firings to complete the resection.

Indocyanine green (ICG) technology is used to identify a well-perfused location for the colorectal anastomosis. The surgeon performs a sigmoid colon resection followed by an end-to-end anastomosis using a powered EEA stapler. An air leak test is conducted, showing no leaks.

The surgeon uses an ultrasound probe to examine both the left and right lobes of the liver, identifying the segment six metastatic lesion. A wedge resection of the lesion is performed using a vessel sealer, followed by ablation of the liver margin to ensure adequate hemostasis.
